name: Babatunde Olatunji
abstract: Michael Babatunde Olatunji (April 7, 1927 – April 6, 2003) was a Nigerian drummer, educator, social activist, and recording artist.

concerts.label: The Sensation of Music at Newport '61: Jon Hendricks' Evolution of the Blues
Midnight Performance: The Sensation of Music at Newport '61/ Jon Hendricks' Evolution of the Blues
Midnight Performance: Dick Gregory with Babatunde Olatunji and His Drums of Passion Company
WLIB Festival of Music and Drama

about_quotes.speaker: Randy Weston
about_quotes.evidence: In this Christian church we had three religions at the same time, through music: Yoruba, Islam, and Christianity... After Babatunde Olatunji performed, then we came out, and then we performed with the Gnawa... the night before that we had Olatunji.
about_quotes.doc_title: Randy Weston — Smithsonian Jazz Oral History

about_quotes.speaker: Cascadu
about_quotes.evidence: Montego was one of those classic Caribbean percussionists, man, who had made it sort of on the big stage with guys like Babatunde Olatunji
about_quotes.doc_title: Cascadu interviewed by Monk Rowe, Clinton, New York, November 15, 2019

about_quotes.speaker: Tyner McCoy
about_quotes.evidence: Yeah the drummers, and of course, what’s his name . . . from Nigeria, conga player Babatunde Olatunji.
about_quotes.doc_title: Tyner McCoy — Smithsonian Jazz Oral History
